free_text: Structure of low-lying states of 56,58FE nuclei by
alpha-particle inelastic scattering.
A.V.F. Radial Ridge cyclotron.
Coupled-channels calculation.
Digitizing error
Digitizing error
The energy resolution was about 150 keV FWHM for 2(1)+,
3- and 4(1)+ states and 250 keV for the other excited
states.
The differential cross sections were normalized to the
prediction of the optical poterntial model for elastic
scattering at the angle later 50 degr. where Coulomb
scattering is dominant.
The target was 99.95% (56Fe) self-supporting metal
foil about 1mg/cm2 thick.
Fig. 2
The target was 99.95% (56Fe) self-supporting metal
foil about 1mg/cm2 thick.
Statistical error from the graphic bars
For 2(1)+ states statistical accuracy was 5% or better
for angles below 40 grad., 3% or better for angles up
to 110 grad and 7% or better at larger angles. For 3-
states statistical accuracy was 15% or better for angle
below 45 grad., 6% or better for angles up to 120 grad.
and not worse than 25% at larger angles.
For 2(2)+ states statistical accuracy ranges between 5%
and 35%. For 4(1)+ state statistical accuracy was 6% or
better. For 4(2)+, 6(1)+ and 6(2)+ statistical accuracy
ranges between 8% and 40%.
Fig. 2
The target was 85.75% (58Fe) self-supporting metal
foils about 1mg/cm2 thick.
Statistical error from graphic bars.
For 2(1)+ states statistical accuracy was 5% or better
for angles below 40 grad., 3% or better for angles up
to 110 grad and 7% or better at larger angles. For 3-
states statistical accuracy was 15% or better for angle
below 45 grad., 6% or better for angles up to 120 grad.
and not worse than 25% at larger angles.
For 2(2)+, 4(1)+,4(2)+ states statistical accuracy
ranges between 5% and 35%.
